title: Phytochemical Screening and Antimicrobial Activity of Syzygium cumini Bark Extracts
abstract: This study investigates the antimicrobial properties of Syzygium cumini (jamun) bark extracts against common oral pathogens. Ethanol and aqueous extracts were prepared and tested against Streptococcus mutans and Lactobacillus acidophilus using agar well diffusion method. The ethanol extract showed significant zones of inhibition comparable to chlorhexidine. Results suggest potential for developing affordable herbal mouthwash formulations for rural dental care programs.
keywords: medicinal plants; antimicrobial; oral health; herbal medicine; Syzygium cumini; traditional knowledge

title: Solar-Powered IoT-Based Water Quality Monitoring System for Rural Tamil Nadu
abstract: Design and deployment of a low-cost solar-powered IoT system for continuous monitoring of drinking water quality parameters including pH, turbidity, dissolved oxygen, and bacterial contamination in rural water sources. The system uses ESP32 microcontrollers with GSM connectivity to send real-time alerts to village panchayat officials when parameters exceed safe limits. Piloted across 5 villages in Erode district with 95% uptime over 6 months.
keywords: IoT; water quality; rural development; solar power; clean water; environmental monitoring

title: Impact of Structured Diabetes Education Program on Glycemic Control Among Rural Patients in Erode District
abstract: A quasi-experimental study evaluating the effectiveness of a structured diabetes education program delivered by trained nursing learners in primary health centres. 120 participants with Type 2 diabetes were enrolled over 6 months. The intervention group showed significant reduction in HbA1c levels (8.2% to 7.1%) compared to control. The program demonstrates that community-based nursing interventions can improve chronic disease outcomes in resource-limited settings.
keywords: diabetes education; community health; nursing intervention; glycemic control; rural healthcare; non-communicable diseases

title: Development of Biodegradable Packaging from Agricultural Waste for Small-Scale Food Enterprises
abstract: This research develops biodegradable food packaging material from sugarcane bagasse and rice husk — abundant agricultural waste in Tamil Nadu. The composite material showed acceptable tensile strength, water resistance, and shelf-life performance for dry food products. Cost analysis indicates the material can be produced at 40% lower cost than conventional biodegradable alternatives, making it viable for small-scale food businesses.
keywords: biodegradable packaging; agricultural waste; sustainable materials; circular economy; food safety; green technology

title: Prevalence and Risk Factors of Early Childhood Caries Among Children in Semi-Urban Areas of Namakkal District
abstract: A cross-sectional study assessing the prevalence of early childhood caries (ECC) among 500 children aged 2-6 years in semi-urban Namakkal. Prevalence was found at 62.4% with significant association to dietary habits, breastfeeding duration, and parental education level. The study recommends school-based preventive programs and maternal health education as cost-effective interventions for reducing childhood oral disease burden.
keywords: early childhood caries; oral health; child health; preventive dentistry; public health; maternal education
